steps.description: Type the text "foundry" into the search input field located at the top of the Samples tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button for the "Microsoft Foundry Agent (TS, Azure, SSO, Agent, Microsoft 365)" sample in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code. Add ${{var:app_name:AzureAgentToM365ATK}} in to env.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
set -e
SRC="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-NodeJS/env/env.TEMPLATE"
DST="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-NodeJS/env/.env.local"

if [ ! -f "$SRC" ]; then
  echo "Source file not found: $SRC" >&2
  exit 1
fi
cp "$SRC" "$DST"
sed -i 's|^AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT=.*|AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT=${{secret:AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT:}}|' "$DST"
sed -i 's|^AGENT_ID=.*|AGENT_ID=${{secret:AGENT_ID:}}|' "$DST"
cat "$DST"
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
# rename to .env
# Prerequired environment variables
AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT=${{secret:AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT:}}
AGENT_ID=${{secret:AGENT_ID:}}



#new variables intoduced in Agent 365 SDK 1.1.1
connections__serviceConnection__settings__clientId= # App ID of the App Registration used to log in.
connections__serviceConnection__settings__clientSecret= # Client secret of the App Registration used to log in
connections__serviceConnection__settings__tenantId= # Tenant ID of the App Registration used to log in

connectionsMap__0__connection=serviceConnection
connectionsMap__0__serviceUrl=*


#To enable debugging for the Agent SDK
DEBUG=agents:*:error

# Logging configuration
# Controls the verbosity of application logs
# Options: error, warn, info (default), debug
# - error: Only critical errors
# - warn: Errors and warnings
# - info: General informational messages (recommended for production)
# - debug: Detailed diagnostic information (for development/troubleshooting)
LOG_LEVEL=info

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code update value of AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT to ${{secret:AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT}}, and value of AGENT_ID=${{secret:AGENT_ID}} for the file "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/ProxyAgent-NodeJS/env/.env.dev"
Click the "Type a message" input box in the Microsoft Teams chat interface to activate the text field for composing a new message.
Type the text "How can you help me?" into the message input box at the bottom of a Microsoft Teams chat window within the "AzureAgentToM365ATKdev" application context.
Press the Enter key to send the message "How can you help me?" in the chat input box within the AzureAgentToM365ATKdev conversation on Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Click the "A+" badge inside the AzureAgentToM365ATKdev app card in the Microsoft Teams web interface to interact with the agent setup or information panel.
Click the "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}}" account entry in the Microsoft "Pick an account" login dialog to select the signed-in user (${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}}).
Click the "Accept" button in the Microsoft Permissions requested dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com web page to grant requested application permissions.
Click the "View prompts" link in the message input area of the Microsoft Teams web interface, triggering prompt suggestions from AzureAgentToM365ATKdev.
Click the "Hotels in the policy" prompt suggestion within the "Prompt Suggestions from AzureAgentToM365ATKdev" popup in the Microsoft Teams chat interface.
Click the "Send" button in the message input area of a Microsoft Teams chat window to submit the question "What are the available hotels?".
